prose:
Method provenance
Property: condensed Fukui functions (electrophilic f⁺, nucleophilic f⁻, dual descriptor) for nitrobenzene; headline value reported here is the largest electrophilic index f⁺.
Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (no density fitting) in PySCF 2.x (chemkit backend)
Geometry provenance: fixed experimental geometry supplied by user as /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/nitrobenzene/nitrobenzene.xyz; the DFT calculation was a single-point on this structure (DFT//exp-xyz), no further optimisation.
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent requested).
Charge and multiplicity: charge 0, multiplicity 1 (closed-shell singlet); N±1 states automatically computed as doublets for f±.
Software: chemkit (fukui-reactivity skill → PySCF engine).
Convergence & warnings: SCF cycles converged for the neutral, cation (charge +1, mult 2) and anion (charge −1, mult 2) states. Integrity status: ok; trustworthy: true. Warnings returned by the engine are reproduced verbatim below.
Live .out log: written during the run to a file named fukui_<timestamp>.out in the working directory (see cli invocation in JSON for exact call); result JSON saved to /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpmwq3ipab/live.json.

Key results (Mulliken-based condensed Fukui indices)
Most electrophilic site: atom 4 (C) with f⁺ = 0.49641779
Most nucleophilic site: atom 5 (C) with f⁻ = 0.10728820
